Package org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty

Class JettyConfiguration

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty.JettyConfiguration
@Configuration public class JettyConfiguration extends Object
@Configuration class that wires Jetty into a Microservice bootstrap.

Add this class to configurations(...) to enable a Jetty-backed microservice:

Microservice .create() .args(args) .configurations(JettyConfiguration.class, MyAppConfig.class) .build() .start() .startConsole() .join();

Contributes the following beans (each gated on @ConditionalOnMissingBean so user-supplied beans of the same type win):

  • JettyServerFactory — defaults to BasicJettyServerFactory, used to build the Jetty Server from jetty.xml.
  • JettySettings — empty defaults; user-supplied @Bean JettySettings can override ports, jetty.xml contents, and var-resolution.
  • JettyServerComponent (always contributed, also registered under MicroserviceListener) — drives the Jetty server lifecycle via the microservice's start() / stop() fan-out.

@Bean Servlet methods in user-supplied @Configuration classes are auto-mounted at Rest.path() during start-up. See JettyServerComponent for full details.

See Also:
  • juneau-microservice-jetty Basics
Since:
10.0.0

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    JettyConfiguration()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    JettyServerComponent
    jettyServerComponent()
    Contributes the JettyServerComponent bean under its own type for direct lookup by external callers.
    JettyServerFactory
    jettyServerFactory()
    Contributes the default Jetty server factory used to build the Server from a jetty.xml file.
    MicroserviceListener
    jettyServerListener()
    Contributes the same JettyServerComponent instance under MicroserviceListener (named jettyServerListener) so it participates in the Microservice.start() / Microservice.stop() fan-out without clobbering any user-supplied unnamed MicroserviceListener.
    JettySettings
    jettySettings()
    Contributes an empty default JettySettings so JettyServerComponent always finds one in the store.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • JettyConfiguration

      public JettyConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • jettyServerFactory

      @ConditionalOnMissingBean(JettyServerFactory.class) public JettyServerFactory jettyServerFactory()
      Contributes the default Jetty server factory used to build the Server from a jetty.xml file.
      Returns:
      The default factory.

    • jettySettings

      @ConditionalOnMissingBean(JettySettings.class) public JettySettings jettySettings()
      Contributes an empty default JettySettings so JettyServerComponent always finds one in the store. Users who need programmatic overrides supply their own @Bean JettySettings.
      Returns:
      The default empty settings.

    • jettyServerComponent

      public JettyServerComponent jettyServerComponent()
      Contributes the JettyServerComponent bean under its own type for direct lookup by external callers.
      Returns:
      The component instance.

    • jettyServerListener

      public MicroserviceListener jettyServerListener()
      Contributes the same JettyServerComponent instance under MicroserviceListener (named jettyServerListener) so it participates in the Microservice.start() / Microservice.stop() fan-out without clobbering any user-supplied unnamed MicroserviceListener.
      Returns:
      The component instance.
